title: Syracuse report urges steps to curb vacant building fires
summary: Syracuse City Auditor Alexander Marion released a 100+ page report titled 'Following the Fire' analyzing 87 structure fires in 2024, of which 25 (approximately one-third) occurred in vacant buildings. The report recommends immediate actions including enhanced property inspections, FEMA-standard labeling of unsafe structures with red 'X' signs, and pattern identification to enable early interventions and prevent future fires that endanger both residents and first responders.

political_signal.description: City Auditor champions data-driven approach to public safety crisis (1/3 of fires in vacant buildings), recommending systematic interventions and establishing inspection standards

processing_notes: Report 'Following the Fire' is primary deliverable and should be cataloged as standalone asset
No budget impacts mentioned; implementation costs for inspection program and signage unknown
Potential CIO relevance: data analytics, GIS mapping for pattern identification, permitting system integration for inspection tracking
FEMA standard labeling suggests intergovernmental coordination opportunity
